$comment: Reference StackRecipe for the acme-main consumer pilot — the hand-authored end-state a saved `docker-compose` ServiceProvisioning.recipe would hold. It realizes the A-rows of the initiative's acme mapping table (env-file materialization, override layering + external network + shared-stack attach, composer install, seed import, cache warmup, migrations, index build, frontend build gate, health loop) and declares the M-row preflights (memory, registry login, mkcert CA, hosts entries, secrets marker) with copy-paste remediation. Validated against stackRecipeSchema by pilot-golden.logic.test.ts. Illustrative — not run against the reduced detection fixtures.

prerequisites.remediation: Log in to the private image registry (e.g. `aws ecr get-login-password | docker login private-registry.example --username AWS --password-stdin`), then re-run this check. Tokens expire ~8-hourly, so a stale login shows up here rather than mid-pull.
Install mkcert and run `mkcert -install` so the `*.acme.local` dev certificates are trusted, then re-run.
Pull the dev secrets into services/app/.env.dev.local via the repo's own secrets step, then re-run — a missing marker means that one-time step has not run yet.
